title: Francis Turner
content:
I could not run or play
        In boyhood.
        In manhood I could only sip the cup,
        Not drink - For scarlet-fever left my heart diseased.
        Yet I lie here
        Soothed by a secret none but Mary knows:
        There is a garden of acacia,
        Catalpa trees, and arbors sweet with vines - 
        There on that afternoon in June
        By Mary's side - 
        Kissing her with my soul upon my lips
        It suddenly took flight.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edgar Lee Masters, titled "Francis Turner", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
